description_long: This object is a large solar panel array, composed of multiple rectangular photovoltaic modules arranged in a grid pattern. The surface is covered with dark blue solar cells, each divided by a fine grid of metallic conductors, and framed by a light-colored border. The array is supported by a central vertical post, likely made of metal, which elevates the entire structure above the ground. The underside reveals a series of smaller support struts or brackets that provide additional stability. The design is typical for ground-mounted solar installations found in urban parks, parking lots, or open community spaces. The overall construction suggests durability and efficient energy capture for urban power generation.
description: A large, grid-patterned solar panel array mounted on a central post, with a dark blue cell surface and metallic framing. The structure is elevated and supported by multiple brackets underneath.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rectangular face of the solar panel array with visible grid lines and metallic frame.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the array, central supporting post, and multiple support brackets beneath the panels.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grid of solar cells and the frame, with less detail of the support structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the central post and the full set of support brackets under the array.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted with steel or aluminum support post and multiple brackets
attribute_array_configuration: single large grid array, approximately 4x8 panels in a rectangular configuration
